text: The surgeon uses a NovaGrasp device to retract the sigmoid colon to the left lateral pelvic sidewall. The peritoneum lateral to the rectum is incised and the incision is lengthened using a monopolar curved scissor. The dissection proceeds anteriorly between the rectum and the vagina, sharply developing the rectovaginal septum without mobilizing posterior to the rectum.

The surgeon trims a piece of Surgimand mesh to size and places it in the abdomen. The mesh is sutured to the distal rectum using interrupted 2-0 Ticron sutures, with additional sutures securing the mesh to the rectum.

The surgeon fixates the mesh to the sacral promontory using interrupted sutures. The tension of the mesh is tested to ensure it is appropriate. Finally, the peritoneum is closed over the mesh with a continuous V-lock suture.
The patient made an uneventful recovery and was discharged home on post-operative day two.
